content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 15, 1995

Larry Barnett
171 West Spain Street
Sonoma, California 95476

Re: Your Request for Assistance
Our File No. A-95-027

Dear Mr. Barnett:

You have requested advice regarding the conflict-of-interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act")¹ as they
pertain to your position as a member of the Sonoma City Council.
The following advice is based upon the facts provided in your
letter and our telephone conversation on March 2, 1995.

QUESTIONS

You are the owner of a bed and breakfast inn in Sonoma and a
member of the Sonoma Valley Visitors' Bureau:

1) May you participate in a city council decision regarding
partial funding for the Sonoma Valley Visitors' Bureau?

2) May you participate in a city council decision regarding
a change in the transit occupancy tax levied by the city?

CONCLUSIONS

1) You may participate in a decision regarding partial
funding for the Sonoma County Visitors' Bureau since there will
not be a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on your
business. However, you may not participate in any funding
decision which will increase or decrease your personal expenses by
$250.

_________________________
¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations, Sections
18000-18954. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 ● (916) 322-5660

File No. A-95-027
Page 2


     2)  You may participate in a decision regarding the city
transit occupancy tax since there will not be a reasonably
foreseeable material financial effect on your business.

                              FACTS —

     You are a city councilmember in the City of Sonoma and the
owner of The Thistle Dew Inn, a bed and breakfast inn in Sonoma.
You are also a member of the Sonoma Valley Visitors' Bureau, a
nonprofit agency which receives approximately twenty percent of
its funding from the City of Sonoma. Your relationship with the
Visitors' Bureau ("Bureau") is no different than that of other
bureau members. You pay a $50 annual membership fee, you purchase
advertising in the Visitors' Bureau Visitors Guide for $900 a
year, you inform the bureau of your room availability and the
volunteers who staff the bureau refer potential guests to your
inn. The bureau's volunteer staff does not directly book any
actual reservations for your inn; all reservations are made by the
individual parties who call, write or stop by. The bureau does
not receive a referral fee for their services.

     You receive no direct remuneration from the bureau nor do you
incur any direct expense to the bureau other than your yearly dues
of $50 and advertising expense of approximately $900. However,
the income to your business as a result of bureau referrals, made
in the normal course of their operation, exceeds $250 in a
calendar year.

     The city council also discusses and establishes several
policies which would affect all bed and breakfast inns in a
uniform manner. The council is considering a change in the
transit occupancy tax levied by the city. This tax is charged in
full to the guest and does not result in any additional net income
to the inn, which pays the tax monthly to the city.

                              ANALYSIS

     Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making,
participating in, or using his official position to influence a
governmental decision in which he knows or has reason to know he
has a financial interest. An official has a financial interest in
a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will
have a material financial effect, distinguishable from its effect
on the public generally, on the official or a member of his
immediate family, or on:

          (a)  Any business entity in which the public
     official has a direct or indirect investment worth
     one thousand dollars ($1,000) or more.

          (b)  Any real property in which the public
     official has a direct or indirect interest worth
     one thousand dollars ($1,000) or more.

File No. A-95-027
Page 3


(c)  Any source of income, other than gifts
and other than loans by a commercial lending
institution in the regular course of business on
terms available to the public without regard to
official status, aggregating two-hundred fifty
dollars ($250) or more in value provided to,
received by or promised to the public official
within 12 months prior to the time when the
decision is made.

(d)  Any business entity in which the public
official is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any position of management.

(e)  Any donor of, or any intermediary or
agent for a donor of, a gift or gifts aggregating
two hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in value
provided to, received by, or promised to the public
official within 12 months prior to the time when
the decision is made.

Section 87103.

Accordingly, you may not make, participate in making, or
attempt to use your official position to influence a governmental
decision if the decision will have a reasonably foreseeable
material financial effect, distinguishable from the effect on the
public generally, on any of your economic interests.  This would
include The Thistle Dew Inn, of which you are the sole owner.

An effect is considered reasonably foreseeable if there is a
substantial likelihood that it will occur.  Certainty is not
required, however, if an effect is only a mere possibility, it is
not reasonably foreseeable.  (In re Thorner (1975) 1 FPPC Ops.198.)

You have asked whether you may participate in a city council
decision regarding partial funding of the visitors' bureau, since
you are a member of the bureau and the bureau refers guests to
your bed and breakfast inn.  You pay an annual membership fee of
$50 and $900 a year for advertising in the visitors' guide.
Volunteers who staff the bureau office may refer potential guests
to your inn, but do not book reservations for your inn.

From the facts you have provided, it does not appear that the
bureau is a source of income to you.  Therefore, under the Act,
you do not have a financial interest which may be affected by the
council's decision regarding funding for the bureau.

However, you may not participate in the decision if it would
increase or decrease your personal expenses by $250.  For example,
if the funding decision would decrease the bureau's funds to the
extent that you would pay an additional $250 for membership and/or
advertising, you may not participate in the decision.  Conversely,
if increased funding for the bureau would result in decreasing

File No. A-95-027
Page 4


your personal expenses for membership and/or advertising by $250,
you may not participate in the decision. (Regulation
18702.1(a)(4), copy enclosed.)

You also inquired if you may participate in a city council
decision regarding a change in the transit occupancy tax.
Regulation 18702.2(g), copy enclosed, sets forth the criteria
which must be analyzed to determine if the decision will have a
material financial effect on your business:

     (1) The decision will result in an increase or
decrease in the gross revenues for a fiscal year of
$10,000 or more; or
     (2) The decision will result in the business
entity incurring or avoiding additional expenses or
reducing or eliminating existing expenses for a fiscal
year in the amount of $2,500 or more; or
     (3) The decision will result in an increase or
decrease in the value of assets or liabilities of
$10,000 or more.

You indicated that the tax is charged in full to the guest
and is paid monthly to the city; the tax does not result in any
additional net income to your inn. Therefore, you may participate
in the decision to change the transit occupancy tax since it will
not result in a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect
on your business.

I trust this answers your questions.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Steven G. Churchwell
                                        General Counsel

                                        By: [illegible]
                                           Jill Stecher
                                           Counsel, Legal Division

SGC:JS:ak

Enclosures
